zh.lifeSummary: 當Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens於0996年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯易維林斯曼-拉-喬利時，他的父親Gautier II de Vexin52歲，母親Alix Senlis44歲。他於1024年4月7日與Godgifu 'Goda'在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1035年8月13日在土耳其易斯坦波爾享年39歲，埋葬在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯布洛-素-梅。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens于0996年出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯易維林斯曼-拉-喬利时，他的父亲Gautier II de Vexin52岁，母亲Alix Senlis44岁。他于1024年4月7日与Godgifu 'Goda'在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1035年8月13日在土耳其易斯坦波爾享年39岁，埋葬在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯布洛-素-梅。

nameMeanings.name: Comte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean Jacques.
French:

nickname for someone in the service of a count or for someone who behaved pretentiously, from Old French conte, cunte ‘count’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lecomte . Compare Conte 3.

variants.name: Lecomte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Monique, Normand, Alain, Armand, Cecile, Dominique, Francois, Germain, Gilles, Gregoire, Huguette, Pierre.

French and Walloon: nickname for someone in the service of a count or for someone who behaved pretentiously, from Old French conte, cunte ‘count’, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Comte 1.
History: This surname is listed (in the form Le Comte) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Vexin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Vane  .

variants.name: Vane
meanings: English (Kent):
variant of   Vanes  .
variant of   Fayne   with southern English voicing of initial [f] to [v].

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wolfgang.
German: possibly a variant of   Aye  .

variants.name: Aye
meanings: North German (Schleswig-Holstein): from a short form of an ancient Germanic (Frisian) personal name with agi ‘point, edge (of a sword)’ as its first element.
Scottish: from a shortened form of the Gaelic personal name Adhamh ‘Adam’.

Burmese: from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, from e ‘cool; calm and quiet’. — Note: Since Burmese do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

nameMeanings.name: d'Amiens
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the personal name Damien, French form of Damian .

variants.name: Damian
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Francisco, Juan, Jose, Pedro, Alberto, Armando, Carlos, Cesar, Elena, Felipe, Lourdes, Luis.

Spanish (Damián), German, Romanian, Italian, and Polish: from the personal name Damián, Damian respectively, Greek Damianos (from damazein ‘to subdue’), Latin Damianus. Saint Damian was an early Christian saint martyred in Cilicia in AD 303 under the emperor Domitian, together with his brother Cosmas (see Kosmas ). In some accounts the brothers are said to have been doctors, and together they were regarded as the patrons of physicians and apothecaries. A later Saint Damian lived in the 7th–8th centuries and was bishop of Pavia; he may have had some influence on the popularity of the personal name in Italy. This surname is also found among Rusyns (Lemkos).
In some cases probably also French (southern France and Lorraine): from a variant of the personal name   Damien  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Dreux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Channel Islands:  See  Druce  .

variants.name: Druce
meanings: Transferred use of the Scottish surname, now used as a given name throughout the English-speaking world. In the 20th century it was particularly popular in Australia. The surname was originally a Norman baronial name, but a precise identification of the place from which it was derived has not been made (there are a large number of possible candidates). The Bruces were an influential Norman family in Scottish affairs in the early Middle Ages; its most famous member was  Robert  ‘the Bruce’ (  1274–1329 ), who is said to have drawn inspiration after his defeat at Methven from the perseverance of a spider in repeatedly climbing up again after being knocked down. He ruled Scotland as  King Robert I  from  1306 to 1329 .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Mantes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Pedro, Armando, Joaquin, Jose Inez, Tomasa.
Altered form of Portuguese   Mendes  .

variants.name: Mendes
meanings: Yiddish form of Hebrew Menahem . It seems to have originated as a result of substitution of the Yiddish diminutive suffix -l (plus an intrusive -d-) for -hem, which was taken erroneously as the German diminutive suffix -chen.

en.lifeSummary: When Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens was born in 0996, in Mantes-la-Jolie, Yvelines, Île-de-France, France, his father, Gautier II de Vexin, was 52 and his mother, Alix Senlis, was 44. He married Godgifu 'Goda' on 7 April 1024, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 13 August 1035, in İstanbul, Türkiye, at the age of 39, and was buried in Boulogne-sur-Mer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens est né en 0996, à Mantes-la-Jolie, Yvelines, Île-de-France, France, son père, Gautier II de Vexin, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Alix Senlis, avait 44 ans. Il a épousé Godgifu 'Goda' le 7 avril 1024, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 13 août 1035, à İstanbul, Turquie, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Boulogne-sur-Mer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens 0996 in Mantes-la-Jolie, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gautier II de Vexin 52 und seine Mutter Alix Senlis 44. Er hat am 7. April 1024 in England, United Kingdom Godgifu 'Goda' geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 13. August 1035 im Alter von 39 Jahren in İstanbul, Türkei und wurde in Boulogne-sur-Mer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens è nato il nel 0996 nel Mantes-la-Jolie, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Gautier II de Vexin, aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Alix Senlis, aveva 44 anni. Lui sposato Godgifu 'Goda' il 7 aprile 1024, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 13 agosto 1035, nel İstanbul, Turchia, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Boulogne-sur-Mer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiensが0996年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスイベラインスマンテス-ラ-ジョリーで生まれた時，父Gautier II de Vexinは52歳，母Alix Senlisは44歳であった。1024年4月7日にイギリスイギリスでGodgifu 'Goda'と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1035年8月13日にトルコイスタンブルで39歳で死去し，フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイスボウログネ-スル-マーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens(이)가 0996년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 이벨라이네스 먼티스-라-조리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gautier II de Vexin(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix Senlis(은)는 44세였다. 그는 1024년 4월 7일 영국 잉글랜드에서 Godgifu 'Goda'(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1035년 8월 13일 튀르키예 이스탠불에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 부로그니-수-머에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens nasceu em 0996, em Mantes-la-Jolie, Yvelines, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Gautier II de Vexin, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Alix Senlis, tinha 44 anos. Ele casou-se com Godgifu 'Goda' em 7 de abril de 1024, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de agosto de 1035, em İstanbul, Turquia, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Boulogne-sur-Mer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens родился в 0996 г. в местности мантес-ла-джоли, ивелаинесь, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Gautier II de Vexin было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Alix Senlis, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Godgifu 'Goda' 7 апреля 1024 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 13 августа 1035 г. в местности истанбул, Турция в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности боулогне-сур-мир, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dreux de Mantes Comte du Vexin et d'Amiens nació en 0996, en Mantes-la-Jolie, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Gautier II de Vexin, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Alix Senlis, tenía 44 años. Se casó con Godgifu 'Goda' el 7 de abril de 1024, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 13 de agosto de 1035, en İstanbul, Turquía, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Boulogne-sur-Mer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.
